title: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD
display_title: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD
wikitext:
[]: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by HARVEST » Tue Aug 23, 2022 11:55 pm So, it is now infamous that Episode 16's original 16mm negatives have been lost, and that the Blu-ray had to be an upscale from a Renewal DVD source taken from a 35mm interpositive. However, this is incompetence from Khara's part, since the restoration for Episode 16 was done in 1080i HD back then for the Renewal DVD. The series has been rebroadcast in other TV stations in Japan using the Renewal version, with some of these broadcasts being in 1080i HD. The Renewal version has been broadcast at least five times in HD: - BS11 (Jan - Jun 2008) - TV Iwate (Jul - Aug 2009) - Yomiuri TV (Jul - Aug 2009) - Nippon TV (Jun - Aug 2009) - Nankai Broadcasting (Jul 2009 - Feb 2010) Now, I am looking across the Internet for an HD broadcast of Episode 16. As these broadcasts may be the only way to watch Episode 16 in native HD. If Khara just went back to the original Renewal 1080i source and deinterlaced it, we would have a much better Episode 16. I am hoping someone can give me leads to find this lost HD version of Episode 16. There may be 1080i broadcast rips from over a decade ago but this may be lost already stuck in dead torrents. We must preserve this episode of Evangelion in the best quality, to prevent this HD version from becoming lost media.

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by deeRez » Sat Aug 27, 2022 6:48 pm You'll probably need to go on Japanese bulletin boards and ask around there for this to be fruitful. Another source to look for is pictures of cels on yahoo auctions and mandarake. If you can find a bunch of cel pictures from the episode it can be used to improve quality.

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Sat Jan 28, 2023 10:31 pm Updated. Have since purchased the Archival boxset. It has what you're looking for, PQ aside.

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by Zeruel48 » Tue Jan 31, 2023 9:00 am brandneweyes wrote: There is an HD version of 16. It was released back in 2003

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Thu Feb 02, 2023 9:18 pm Windows key+PrtScr is not considered Post-processing. That said, for reason(s) unbeknownst to me, Snapshot (PowerDVD14) does not accurately capture what's displayed onscreen, despite hitting pause. Because of this, I employ Windows key+PrtScr, which I then downscale. The latter half can be considered Post-processing.

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Feb 08, 2023 10:40 am brandneweyes wrote: Hello. Now don't get upset, because my intent isn't to demean, but you sound like a typical Western fan. There is an HD version of 16. It was released back in 2003 along with the other episodes.

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Wed Feb 08, 2023 2:26 pm Updated. Archives of Evangelion. Effectively replaces the '97 jewel DVDs, as well as the '99 Theatrical films release (D&R).

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Feb 08, 2023 10:42 pm brandneweyes wrote: Yeah. But "...BD resolution.", is only as good as the video.

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Wed Feb 08, 2023 11:16 pm Correct. But the Renewal of 16 offers more than fantastic video, with equal stunning color and detail, as my provided screen captures clearly show, crushed blacks aside. I know Windows+PrtScr captures the monitor's resolution, not the video, but in 480 on an LED television, you'll think it was 4k.

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Feb 09, 2023 9:20 am brandneweyes wrote: Correct. But the Renewal of 16 offers more than fantastic video, with equal stunning color and detail, as my provided screen captures clearly show, crushed blacks aside. I know Windows+PrtScr captures the monitor's resolution, not the video, but in 480 on an LED television, you'll think it was 4k.

[]: Re: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Thu Feb 09, 2023 9:38 am Updated. Yeah. I'm an idiot, Archive's of Evangelion aside.

[]: The Hunt for Episode 16 in HD             Quote Post by HARVEST » Tue Aug 23, 2022 11:55 pm So, it is now infamous that Episode 16's original 16mm negatives have been lost, and that the Blu-ray had to be an upscale from a Renewal DVD source taken from a 35mm interpositive. However, this is incompetence from Khara's part, since the restoration for Episode 16 was done in 1080i HD back then for the Renewal DVD. The series has been rebroadcast in other TV stations in Japan using the Renewal version, with some of these broadcasts being in 1080i HD. The Renewal version has been broadcast at least five times in HD: - BS11 (Jan - Jun 2008) - TV Iwate (Jul - Aug 2009) - Yomiuri TV (Jul - Aug 2009) - Nippon TV (Jun - Aug 2009) - Nankai Broadcasting (Jul 2009 - Feb 2010) Now, I am looking across the Internet for an HD broadcast of Episode 16. As these broadcasts may be the only way to watch Episode 16 in native HD. If Khara just went back to the original Renewal 1080i source and deinterlaced it, we would have a much better Episode 16. I am hoping someone can give me leads to find this lost HD version of Episode 16. There may be 1080i broadcast rips from over a decade ago but this may be lost already stuck in dead torrents. We must preserve this episode of Evangelion in the best quality, to prevent this HD version from becoming lost media.
